posts.text:
Disrupting Gulf LNG supplies leads to *worse* pollution outcomes because the alternative is to burn more coal. 

Wonder if this could have any lessons for American green NGOs’ bizarre war against US LNG exports?


Matthew Zeitlin (@MattZeitlin)

great overview of impact of the Hormuz closure on LNG from @ira_joseph. to the extent substitution can happen in the power sector, it looks like it will be coal picking up the slack (along with higher prices and demand destruction) energypolicy.columbia.

posts.text:
From 50,000 feet I think it's easy to post on the internet "we need to return to real state capacity like in the New Deal instead of this endless reliance on contracting and outsourcing." 

But contracting is a very convenient workaround to public sector pay compression, unions, job tenure, etc
